questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question: In the law of simple machine, $\mathrm{P} = \mathrm{mW} + \mathrm{C}$, the constant C denotes
options: effort lost in friction under load W
effort lost in friction under no load
effort lost in friction when M.A. is maximum
questions.hint: C is the constant effort needed to overcome machine friction.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question: If efficiency of a lifting machine is kept constant, then it's V.R. is directly proportional to it's
questions.hint: $\eta = \frac{MA}{VR}$.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question: The law of wheel and axle machine is $\mathrm{P} = 0.2\mathrm{W} + 50$. The diameter of wheel is $600\mathrm{mm}$ and radius of axle is $50\mathrm{mm}$. Determine (a) Mechanical Advantage, (b) Effort required to lift a load of 2000 N, (c) Maximum Efficiency, (d) Maximum Mechanical Advantage and (e) Load to be lifted by an effort of 520 N.
questions.hint: $VR = \frac{D}{d}$ and $MA = \frac{W}{P}$.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question:
(a) What is the relationship between effort and the load lifted of a simple lifting machine? Plot the graph of effort vs load lifted for actual machine.
(b) In a lifting machine, the effort of $15\mathrm{N}$ lifted a load of $700\mathrm{N}$. Find the velocity ratio (V.R.) if the efficiency at this load is $60\%$. If a load of $1300\mathrm{N}$ is lifted by an effort of $25\mathrm{N}$ by the same machine, find the law of the machine. What will be effort required to lift the load of $1000\mathrm{N}$? Determine maximum Mechanical Advantage (M.A.) and maximum efficiency.
questions.hint: Use $\eta = \frac{MA}{VR}$ and assume law of machine is linear $P = mW + C$.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question:
(a) Deduce the condition for self-locking of a machine.
(b) A load of $2\mathrm{kN}$ is to be lifted by a screw-jack, having threads of $10\mathrm{mm}$ pitch. The efficiency of the jack at load is $50\%$. Determine the effort applied at the end of the handle of $50\mathrm{cm}$ length.
questions.hint: For a screw jack, $VR = \frac{2\pi L}{p}$ and $\eta = \frac{MA}{VR}$.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question: Define mechanical advantage, velocity ratio and efficiency of a machine.
questions.hint: MA = Load/Effort, VR = Distance moved by effort/Distance moved by load, Efficiency = MA/VR.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question: What are the advantages of a lifting machine?
questions.hint: They multiply force, change direction of effort, etc.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question: Write the general expression of Mechanical Advantage (MA) & Velocity Ratio (VR) of simple lifting machine. Deduce the condition of reversibility of a machine. What do you mean by non-reversible or self-locking machine?
questions.hint: A machine is reversible if efficiency > 50%.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question: Write the expression of VR of a simple screw jack. The efficiency of a lifting machine is $80\%$ when an effort of $50 \mathrm{N}$ is required to lift a load of $1 \mathrm{kN}$. Find the mechanical advantage and velocity ratio of the machine.
questions.hint: $VR = \frac{2\pi L}{p}$, $MA = \frac{W}{P}$, $\eta = \frac{MA}{VR}$.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question: What is Reversible & Non-reversible machine?
questions.hint: Reversible if efficiency > 50%, non-reversible if efficiency ≤ 50%.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question: What do you mean by 'effort lost in friction' and 'frictional resistance'?
questions.hint: Effort lost in friction is extra effort to overcome friction.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question: The law of wheel and axle machine is $\mathrm{P} = 0.2\mathrm{W} + 50$. The diameter of wheel is $600\mathrm{mm}$ and radius of axle is $50\mathrm{mm}$. Determine (a) Mechanical Advantage, (b) Effort required to lift a load of $2000\mathrm{N}$, (c) Maximum Efficiency, (d) Maximum Mechanical Advantage and (e) Load to be lifted by an effort of $520\mathrm{N}$.
questions.hint: $VR = \frac{D}{d}$ and $MA = \frac{W}{P}$.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question:
(a) Define (i) Angle of Friction, (ii) Angle of Repose.
(b) What load will be lifted by an effort of 12 N, if the velocity ratio is 18 and efficiency of the machine at this load is $60\%$? If the machine has a constant friction resistance, determine the law of the machine.
questions.hint: For (b), use $\eta = \frac{MA}{VR}$ and law of machine $P = mW + C$.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question: The velocity ratio of a simple wheel and axle with 'D' and 'd' as the diameters of effort wheel and load axle, is
questions.hint: $VR = \frac{D}{d}$.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question: In simple lifting machine, Load vs. Effort graph is -
questions.hint: For a simple machine, load-effort relationship is linear.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question: In a simple lifting machine, the following parameter remains constant -
questions.hint: VR is constant for an ideal machine, but changes with friction.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question: A lifting machine is said to be reversible if its efficiency is _______ than $50\%$.
questions.hint: Reversible if efficiency > 50%.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question: Efficiency of a self- locking lifting machine is _______.
questions.hint: Self-locking if efficiency < 50%.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question: In actual machines, Mechanical advantage is _______ than velocity ratio.
questions.hint: MA is less than VR due to friction.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question: What is law of machine?
questions.hint: It is the relation between load and effort.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question: What is an ideal machine?
questions.hint: Machine with no friction, efficiency = 100%.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question:
(a) Find the centroid of the cross section shown in Figure 7(a). All dimensions are in mm.
(b) What is the minimum effort $P$ required to lift a load $W$ by a screw jack when helix angle is $\alpha$ and coefficient of friction is $\tan \phi$ where $\phi$ is the angle of friction? A simple screw jack has a thread of pitch of $10 \mathrm{mm}$, find the mechanical advantage and efficiency, if an effort of $20 \mathrm{N}$ is applied at the end of an arm $500 \mathrm{mm}$ long to lift a load $2.6 \mathrm{kN}$.
(c) A wheel rotating about a fixed axis at 20 revolutions per minute is uniformly accelerated for 70 sec. during which it makes 50 revolutions. Find the (i) angular velocity at the end of this interval and (ii) time required for the velocity to reach 100 revolutions per minute.
questions.hint: For screw jack, $MA = \frac{W}{P}$, $VR = \frac{2\pi L}{p}$.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question: If the law of lifting machine is $\mathrm{P} = 0.04\mathrm{W} + 20$ the maximum mechanical advantage is 25.
questions.hint: Maximum MA = $\frac{1}{m}$ when C is zero.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question: Mechanical advantage is the ability of a machine to multiply force.
questions.hint: MA = Load/Effort.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question: In case of a lifting machine load vs. effort graph is a curve.
questions.hint: For simple machines, it is a straight line.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question: Instead of simple wheel and axle, differential wheel and axle gives _______ V.R.
questions.hint: Higher VR than simple wheel and axle.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question: In the equation $\mathrm{P} = \mathrm{mW} + \mathrm{C}$ of a lifting machine, C indicates the effort required to overcome friction under _______.
questions.hint: C is effort at no load.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question: If efficiency of a lifting machine is kept constant, its velocity ratio is directly proportional to its _______.
questions.hint: $\eta = \frac{MA}{VR}$.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question:
(a) Define velocity ratio, mechanical advantage and efficiency of a simple lifting machine.
(b) In a differential wheel and axle, the diameter of the effort wheel is $40cm$. The radii of the axles are $15cm$ and $10cm$ respectively. Find the load which can be lifted by an effort of $100N$, assuming the efficiency of the machine to be $75\%$.
questions.hint: $VR = \frac{2D}{d_1 - d_2}$, $\eta = \frac{MA}{VR}$.

questions.subject: mechanics
questions.question: In a simple machine, whose velocity ratio is 40, a load of $1500N$ is lifted by an effort of $100N$ and a load of $2500N$ is lifted by an effort of $160N$. Find the law of machine. Also calculate the load that could be lifted by an effort of $200N$.
questions.hint: Assume $P = mW + C$ and solve using given data.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: A galvanometer can be converted into an ammeter by connecting it with a (a) suitable high resistance in parallel (b) suitable low resistance in parallel (c) suitable high resistance in series (d) suitable low resistance in series
questions.hint: To measure large currents, most of the current must bypass the coil via a shunt.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: Magnetic dipole moment of a current carrying loop is independent of (a) number of turns in the coil (b) area of the loop of the coil (c) current in the coil (d) magnetic field in which it is placed
options: number of turns in the coil
area of the loop of the coil
magnetic field in which it is placed
questions.hint: The magnetic dipole moment depends on the loop's properties, not the external field.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: (a) Define magnetic flux. What are its SI units?
questions.hint: It is the dot product of magnetic field and area vector. Unit is Weber (Wb).

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: (b) State Biot-Savart's law and apply it to find the magnetic field at the centre of a circular loop carrying current I.
questions.hint: Integrate the field contribution from each current element. At the centre, the field is perpendicular to the plane and adds up.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: (c) State Lenz's law. Does it obey the principle of conservation of energy?
questions.hint: The induced emf opposes the change causing it, ensuring energy conservation.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: The weber/m² (Tesla) is the unit of (a) magnetic field (b) magnetic flux density (c) magnetic flux (d) none of these
questions.hint: $1 \text{ T} = 1 \text{ Wb/m}^2$.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: (a) State Biot-Savart law. Write the expression for magnetic field due to a straight current-carrying wire.
questions.hint: $d\vec{B} = \frac{\mu_0}{4\pi} \frac{I d\vec{l} \times \hat{r}}{r^2}$. For infinite wire, $B = \frac{\mu_0 I}{2\pi r}$.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: (b) Find the magnetic field at the centre of a circular coil of radius 0.1 m carrying current 5A \((\mu_{0} = 4\pi \times 10^{-7}\mathrm{H / m})\) .
questions.hint: $B = \frac{\mu_0 I}{2R}$ for a single loop.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: (c) State Ampere's circular law.
questions.hint: $\oint \vec{B} \cdot d\vec{l} = \mu_0 I_{enc}$.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: (a) State Faraday's laws of electromagnetic induction. Write the expression for induced emf.
questions.hint: Emf is induced when magnetic flux changes. $\mathcal{E} = -\frac{d\Phi}{dt}$.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: (b) A coil of 100 turns has a magnetic flux of 0.02 Wb. If the flux is reduced to zero in 0.1 seconds, find the induced emf.
questions.hint: Induced emf = $-N \frac{\Delta \Phi}{\Delta t}$.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: (c) State Lenz's law. How does it obey the law of conservation of energy?
questions.hint: The induced emf opposes the change, ensuring energy is not created out of nothing.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: (a) State Lenz's law. Does it obey the principle of conservation of energy?
questions.hint: Induced emf opposes change. Yes, it ensures energy conservation.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: (b) Deduce an expression for the magnitude of the magnetic field at the centre of a circular coil of N turns.
questions.hint: $B = \frac{\mu_0 N I}{2R}$ for the centre of a circular coil.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: (c) A straight wire carrying current of 12A is bent into a semi-circular arc of radius 0.02m. Find the magnitude of the magnetic field at the centre of the arc.
questions.hint: $B = \frac{\mu_0 I}{4R}$ for a semi-circular arc.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: (a) State Biot-Savart law and express this law in the vector form.
questions.hint: $d\vec{B} = \frac{\mu_0}{4\pi} \frac{I d\vec{l} \times \hat{r}}{r^2}$.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: (b) An electron travelling with velocity \(30~\mathrm{cm s}^{-1}\) enters a region of uniform magnetic field \(0.2\mathrm{T}\) acting perpendicular to its paths. Calculate the magnitude of force on the electron. (charge of electron \(= 1.6\times 10^{-19}\mathrm{C}\) )
questions.hint: $F = q v B \sin\theta$. Here, $\theta=90^\circ$.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: (c) State and explain Lenz's law.
questions.hint: The direction of induced emf is such that it opposes the change causing it.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: (d) A galvanometer of resistance \(100\Omega\) gives full scale deflection when a current of \(500\mu \mathrm{A}\) passes through it. How can this be converted to a voltmeter reading up to \(5\mathrm{V}\) ? Draw necessary circuit diagram.
questions.hint: Connect a high resistance in series with the galvanometer. $R_{series} = \frac{V}{I_g} - R_g$.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: The formula for the magnetic field inside a long solenoid of length L & total number of turns N, carrying a current I is (a) \(B = \mu_{0} n I\) (b) \(B = \mu_{0} N I / L\) (c) \(B = \mu_{0} N I / L\) (d) None of these
questions.hint: $B = \mu_0 n I$, where $n = \frac{N}{L}$.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: Lenz's law in electromagnetic induction is another form of the law of (a) Conservation of energy (b) Conservation of charge (c) Conservation of mass (d) None of these
questions.hint: Opposing the change ensures energy is not created from nothing.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: Which of the following does not exist? (a) Static charge (b) Moving charge (c) Magnetic dipole (d) Magnetic monopole
questions.hint: Magnetic monopoles have not been observed in nature.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: The SI unit of magnetic flux density is
questions.hint: Tesla (T).

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: In a moving coil galvanometer is produced to rotate the pointer.
questions.hint: Magnetic torque.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: If a static charge is placed inside a magnetic field, will there be any force acting on it?
questions.hint: No, because $v = 0$.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: Why induced emf is also called back emf?
questions.hint: It opposes the change in current that produced it.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: Two parallel wires carry same current in same direction. What will happen?
questions.hint: They attract each other.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: What will happen if a very high resistance, instead of a low resistance, is connected in parallel to a galvanometer to convert it into an ammeter?
questions.hint: The ammeter will not work properly as most current will still pass through the coil.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: 1) State and explain Ampere's circuital law.
questions.hint: $\oint \vec{B} \cdot d\vec{l} = \mu_0 I_{enc}$. It relates magnetic field to current.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: b) A long solenoid has 500 turns per meter and carries a current of 4A. Calculate the magnetic field inside the solenoid.
questions.hint: $B = \mu_0 n I$, where $n =$ number of turns per unit length.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: c) What is the difference between diamagnetic and paramagnetic materials?
questions.hint: Diamagnetic: repelled by field, $\mu < 1$. Paramagnetic: weakly attracted, $\mu > 1$.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: a) Explain the working principle of a transformer.
questions.hint: Mutual induction between two coils.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: b) A step-up transformer has 200 turns on primary and 1200 turns on secondary. If the primary voltage is 230V, find the secondary voltage.
questions.hint: $\frac{V_s}{V_p} = \frac{N_s}{N_p}$.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: c) What are eddy currents? How can they be minimized?
questions.hint: Circulating currents in a conductor in a changing magnetic field. Minimized by laminating the core.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: A moving charge produces (a) an electric field only (b) a magnetic field only (c) Both electric and magnetic field (d) either electric field or magnetic field
options: either electric field or magnetic field
questions.hint: A moving charge is a current, which produces a magnetic field in addition to its electric field.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: The SI unit of magnetic flux is (a) weber (b) gauss (c) oersted (d) none of these
questions.hint: Weber (Wb).

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: The induced emf in a 1 millihreny inductor in which the current changes from 5A to 3A in \(10^{- 3}\) s is (a) \(2\times 10^{-6}\mathrm{V}\) (b) \(8\times 10^{-6}\mathrm{V}\) (c) 2V (d) 8V
questions.hint: $\mathcal{E} = -L \frac{\Delta I}{\Delta t}$.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: If a conductor moves across a magnetic field, the direction of induced emf is obtained from (a) Laplace's law (b) Fleming's left hand rule (c) Fleming's right hand rule (d) none of these
questions.hint: Fleming's right-hand rule gives the direction of induced current.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: a) State Biot-Savart's law in vector form expressing the magnetic field due to an element carrying current at a distance from the element. A circular coil of radius R carries a current I. Derive the expression for the magnetic field due to this coil at its centre. What is the effect of increasing the number of turns on magnetic field produced to a circular coil?
questions.hint: $B$ at centre $= \frac{\mu_0 I}{2R}$ for single loop. For $N$ turns, $B = \frac{\mu_0 N I}{2R}$.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: b) State the Faraday's law of electromagnetic induction.
questions.hint: Induced emf is proportional to the rate of change of magnetic flux.

questions.subject: physics
questions.question: c) A galvanometer of resistance \(50\Omega\) gives full scale deflection when a current of \(500\mu \mathrm{A}\) passes through it. It is to be converted into a voltmeter reading up to 10V. Explain how this can be done? Circuit diagram is essential.
questions.hint: Connect a high resistance in series. $R = \frac{V}{I_g} - R_g$.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a web browser?
questions.hint: Web browsers are used to access and view websites.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a function of an operating system?
questions.hint: Compiling is done by a compiler, not the OS core.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: What is operating system? Explain functions of an Operating System.
questions.hint: It manages hardware and software resources.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: What are the differences between multiprogramming and time-sharing system?
questions.hint: Time-sharing is an extension of multiprogramming with user interaction.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: Operating Systems used in the computer system for
questions.hint: OS handles all resources.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: Free and efficient operating system is
questions.hint: UNIX and Linux are open source.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: Explain the advantages of multi-user and multitasking OS.
questions.hint: Efficient resource utilization.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: Describe the general architecture of an operating system.
questions.hint: Kernel, shell, system calls, etc.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: What is Booting?
questions.hint: Starting the computer.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: Give some importance functions of Operating System.
questions.hint: Memory, process, file, device management.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: What is the difference between Batch Processing System and Realtime Processing System?
questions.hint: Batch processes jobs in groups, realtime responds immediately.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: Write the main difference between Multitasking and Multiprogramming.
questions.hint: Multitasking involves user interaction, multiprogramming does not.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: CPU scheduling is the basis of
questions.hint: Allows multiple programs to run concurrently.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: Which of the following errors will be handled by the operating system?
questions.hint: OS manages hardware and resources.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: The Operating System is responsible for interacting with ______
questions.hint: Hardware and users.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: In ______ scheduling, each process is given a small amount of time to execute.
questions.hint: Time slicing.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: ______ is a batch file stored in the root directory of the hard-disk.
questions.hint: Autoexec.bat.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: Define Booting.
questions.hint: Starting the computer.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: Is it possible to run a computer without operating system? Write your opinion.
questions.hint: Very difficult, embedded systems may run without full OS.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: Write the utility of Command Line Interpreter.
questions.hint: Accepts and executes user commands.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: Write the advantages of multiprocessing operating system.
questions.hint: Increased throughput, reliability, parallel processing.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a Real Time Operating System
questions.hint: Used in embedded systems.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: Which of the following is not an operating system?
questions.hint: Oracle is a database system.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: What is operating system?
questions.hint: Interface between user and hardware.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: What are the basic services provided by the operating system? What is CPU and I/O bound jobs?
questions.hint: Process, memory, file, I/O management.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: What is time sharing operating system?
questions.hint: Multiple users share CPU time.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: What are the advantages of multiprocessor system?
questions.hint: Increased throughput, reliability.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: What is a bootstrap program in OS? What is Batch Operating System?
questions.hint: Bootstrap loads OS, batch processes jobs in groups.

questions.subject: it
questions.question: Write a short note on Types of OS.
questions.hint: Batch, time-sharing, real-time, distributed.
